title: Projects
text:
Completed Projects

Evolution of Life-History Traits
A theoretical investigation of the trade-offs between growth rate, fecundity, and survival that shape life-history strategies across biological taxa. Published results are available via the Publications page.


Status: Completed


 Publication  Repository

title: Projects
text:
Linking External Repositories
This lab website is designed to link directly to other repositories within the Pichugin-Lab GitHub organization. As new repositories are made public they will be featured here with descriptions, documentation links, and usage examples.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Understanding the evolutionary dynamics of Malaria
View Publications Lab Resources

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Research Overview
Our laboratory investigates the evolutionary and ecological processes that shape Malaria antigenic and genomic diversity.


Plasmodium falciparum
We develop and analyze mathematical models of natural selection, genetic drift, and eco-evolutionary dynamics.


Phylogenetics
We build simulation frameworks to explore the emergence of cooperation, altruism, and social behavior.


Open Science
We make our code, data, and analyses publicly available to support reproducible research. Explore our GitHub organization.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Latest News

2025 — New preprint on multilevel selection dynamics posted to bioRxiv.
2024 — Lab GitHub organization launched. Repositories for ongoing projects are now publicly accessible.
2024 — New resources and tutorial materials added to the Resources page.

title: Pichugin Lab
text:
Join the Lab
We welcome motivated students and postdocs interested in evolutionary theory, mathematical biology, and computational modeling. See the About page for contact information and open positions.

title: About
text:
Dr. Alexander Pichugin, MD, PhD
ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2577-7284 SciProfiles: https://sciprofiles.com/profile/author/dmF6RThpK2xYdHpQZDkzQ2RYZlNtNUxPdDlJRlB0VDRIcW1naXFLSU5YQXNFZWl6RXFjeXZndTJ3RE5QS1lacQ==
Department of [Your Department], [Your Institution]
I am a researcher studying evolutionary dynamics, cooperation, and the mathematical principles underlying biological complexity. My work draws on tools from dynamical systems, probability theory, and computer simulation to address questions about how life evolves and organizes at multiple scales.

title: About
text:
Research Interests

Mathematical modeling of evolutionary processes
Multilevel selection and major evolutionary transitions
Cooperation and the evolution of social behavior
Population genetics and eco-evolutionary dynamics
Computational approaches to theoretical biology
